mission.title: traefik: Healthchecks for TCP forwarded connections
mission.description: Healthchecks for TCP forwarded connections. This issue affects 39+ users.

steps.title: Identify traefik feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your traefik deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=traefik
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `msg="Error while connection to backend: dial tcp 172.25.0.3:6443: connect: connection refused"`

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
Traefik forward connections even if there is no open TCP port.
In general there are a lot of cases when you may export more then one TCP port from single container or when container is up but specific

steps.title: Apply the fix for Healthchecks for TCP forwarded connections
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?
When no up servers left in a service, exclude it from WRR to avoid 503-s.

### Motivation

### More

### Additional Notes
```yaml
traefik version
Version:      2.0.1
Codename:     montdor
Go version:   go1.13.1
Built:        2019-09-26T16:18:03Z
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

steps.title: Confirm Healthchecks for TCP forwarded connections is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `msg="Error while connection to backend: dial tcp 172.25.0.3:6443: connect: connection refused"` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ### What does this PR do?
When no up servers left in a service, exclude it from WRR to avoid 503-s.

### Motivation

### More

### Additional Notes. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
traefik version
Version:      2.0.1
Codename:     montdor
Go version:   go1.13.1
Built:        2019-09-26T16:18:03Z
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

version: '3.0'                                                                                                                           services:
  lb:
    hostname: lb
    image: traefik:latest
    command:
      - --api.insecure=true
      - --providers.docker
      - --entryPoints.http.address=:80
      - --entryPoints.kubeapi.address=:6443
      - --log.level=DEBUG
    ports:
      - "6443:6443"
      - "8080:8080"
      - "80:80"
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=false"
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
  node1:
    build:
      context: ./config
    hostname: node1
    image: kube
    labels:
      - traefik.tcp.routers.kube-apiserver.entrypoints=kubeapi
      - traefik.tcp.routers.kube-apiserver.rule=Ho

lb_1     | time="2019-10-08T12:25:36Z" level=error msg="Error while connection to backend: dial tcp 172.25.0.3:6443: connect: connection refused"

lb_1     | time="2019-10-08T12:25:36Z" level=debug msg="Handling connection from 172.25.0.4:54850"
lb_1     | time="2019-10-08T12:25:36Z" level=debug msg="Error while terminating connection: close tcp 172.25.0.2:6443->172.25.0.4:54850: shutdown:
transport endpoint is not connected"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
